programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Avery Ryan's old friend Jordan Tan seeks her help in locating his missing teenage daughter Zoey. When the team looks into high-schooler's online history, they discover she had been the target of hacks, faked phone calls and social media posts, as well as a malicious website titled 'Kill Yourself Zoey Tan'. The spate of abuse bears all the hallmarks of a particularly nasty case of online bullying, but Zoey's digital footprint offers the investigators a lead in the form of online boyfriend Owen, who lives 300 miles away and seems to have invited her to visit. However, when the team ventures to Owen's home, they discover his invitation was just another facet of the hate campaign, and with Zoey's whereabout still unknown, serious concerns are raised about her wellbeing. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, with guests Byron Mann and BooBoo Stewart.(n)

programme.sub-title: When Push Comes to Shove - Part Two
programme.desc: The second series of the paranormal drama picks up from the first season's cliffhanger finale. As Captain Push lies in a coma, Allison tries to decipher the clues in her visions and uncover the identity of the serial killer.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Avery Ryan's old friend Jordan Tan seeks her help in locating his missing teenage daughter Zoey. When the team looks into high-schooler's online history, they discover she had been the target of hacks, faked phone calls and social media posts, as well as a malicious website titled 'Kill Yourself Zoey Tan'. The spate of abuse bears all the hallmarks of a particularly nasty case of online bullying, but Zoey's digital footprint offers the investigators a lead in the form of online boyfriend Owen, who lives 300 miles away and seems to have invited her to visit. However, when the team ventures to Owen's home, they discover his invitation was just another facet of the hate campaign, and with Zoey's whereabout still unknown, serious concerns are raised about her wellbeing. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, with guests Byron Mann and BooBoo Stewart.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker tries to prove the innocence of a boy with learning difficulties who has been accused of murder.(n)

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: Part two of two. A lawyer stands accused of murder when his client dies under suspicious circumstances.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy bargains galore.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy officer scheduled to testify in a forthcoming murder trial goes missing after receiving a threat at the safe house where he was staying, and the deputy district attorney asks the NCIS agents to locate him. One of their few leads is a dead turkey Tony and McGee find in the man's fridge, so they take it to Ducky for an autopsy. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit).(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Return of the military drama. The team is reunited when a nuclear-powered satellite is knocked out of orbit. Meanwhile, Paige and Walter confront their feelings.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A woman from Cabe's past asks for help in apprehending a Serbian war criminal, so the team travels to Cuba to track him down. Walter faces a judge following his car accident.(n)

programme.title: The High Chaparral
programme.sub-title: Gold Is Where You Leave It
programme.desc: When three men believe they have found gold on the High Chaparral, Big John orders the area destroyed. The trio decide to fight back, putting the Cannons in danger and prompting Buck to have conflicted feelings about Victoria.(n)

programme.title: The High Chaparral
programme.desc: The Cannons look after an injured relative, not realising he is wanted for murder.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah reveals her horrific secret to George, and their relationship is suddenly thrown into doubt. The Christmas soiree brings out Jack's intimacy issues, and Anna publicly humiliates Andrew. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: To Catch a Killer - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of three. Jack is forced to arrest his own son Benjamin for possession of drugs, and the corpulent form of convicted rapist Peter Baxter again crosses Sam's path as she helps Ramani investigate a nurse's disappearance. Gabriel invites Sheelagh back to his place in a bid to prove that he has finally managed to forget about Kerry Young, while Yvonne is summoned to hear her fate as the inquiry into the death of Lee Chapman concludes.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Helena's post-mortem reveals the error made during Nick's surgery, but Diane realises unless the surgeon comes clean, she could be the one to take the blame. Joseph's condition grows noticeably worse, and a prison inmate is admitted to the hospital after being injured in a riot. Former EastEnders actor Todd Carty guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Sean is devastated to discover the truth about Max and Tina, and Adam feels pressured to reveal his secret. Meanwhile, Patrick is amazed when a blind man inexplicably regains his sight - could it be a miracle? Medical drama, starring Gerald Kyd, Pal Aron and Ian Kelsey.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: To Catch a Killer - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of three. Peter Baxter is released on bail, even though there is evidence linking him to the disappearance of nurse Manisha Devi - but the subsequent discovery of her burnt body raises the possibility that a dreadful mistake has been made. Samantha presses ahead with the investigation, and identifies another suspect in the form of taxi driver David Russo. Unfortunately, she isn't aware that Ramani is already in the back of his cab, being driven through the streets of Sun Hill by someone who may be a killer.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon and Grant are on a romantic weekend and Phil and Kathy are getting to know each other better. Meanwhile Frank and Pat are visiting Diane who is heavily pregnant.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Richard goes green with jealousy when he sees Phil and Kathy together, Nigel confronts Nick as he tries to steal his mothers furniture and Arthur comes clean about his double life.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Angry protestors plant a fire-bomb in a chemist's shop, unaware the manager is still on the premises. Billy finally meets his mother, and Hallam fends off lovelorn student Jenny.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Lexie is annoyed when her estranged father arrives in Glenbogle, but he soon sets about charming the rest of the MacDonald clan with his tall stories. Molly and Golly pose as a married couple to buy a horse, but are forced to keep up the pretence for longer than they anticipated, and Duncan's plans to impress Jess go awry when it becomes clear they have very different tastes in interior decoration.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The locals bid an emotional farewell to Vic and Lucy, and Simon's problems continue to mount as his business crumbles and he is consumed by guilt over his fling with Evelyn. Elsewhere, a dishy gardener steals the heart of a wheelchair-bound patient, while Tom and Beth take advantage of a drunken Joe's incapacity and steal a kiss. Rural drama, starring Thomas Craig and Tony Haygarth.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The sisters face up to the realities of life with their husbands in prison, and try to explain things to Tracey's son, Garth. Classic comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene allows Boycie to buy a maximum of six cows for the farm - and is less than impressed when Elgin, Jed and Bryan turn up with a herd of 300. The vet is called out when Rocky the bull fails to take any notice of the females, and Tyler prepares to sit a local private school's entrance exam. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Lionel is concerned that he has no pension plans, so the ever-resourceful Alistair comes up with an ingenious idea - to sell the country house. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo's nephew is getting married, and while the groom's uncle considers having a bath to mark the occasion, Foggy fears disaster will befall the happy couple.(n)

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The judge presides over the case of a man who killed a paedophile, an act that Jo defends by arguing it protected further victims from abuse. Meanwhile, she meets the real father of the boy she is trying to adopt, and begins seeing him socially - sending Deed into a fit of jealous rage. Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove star.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the murder of a man found in an alleyway seven years previously when he is identified on a website covering unexplained disappearances. The victim is named by a woman whose husband hired him six months before his death, but neither of them had thought anything of it when their employee went missing - and seem to know little about him. Meanwhile, Brian and Gerry are distracted by looming job cuts and fear that Ucos could be downsized. With Robert Daws and Claudie Blakley.(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A woman who went missing from a care home in 1986 is found murdered in a burnt-out car, and the team discovers she had recently abducted a teenage girl herself. As the investigation continues, both cases appear to be linked to an urban myth about a character known as the Bag Man - an eyeless villain who kidnaps children. With Eva Birthistle, Tara FitzGerald and Wil Johnson.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Sequel to The White Queen, set in the Middle Ages and picking up as the tumultuous War of the Roses comes to an end, a new generation ascends to the throne in a tale of power, political clashes, family, love and betrayal. As the victor of the Battle of Bosworth, Henry Tudor, is set to ascend the throne and Elizabeth of York and her family, supporters of the former King, find themselves in grave danger. Lizzie must fulfil her promise to marry King Henry VII and bring peace to the nation. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: King Henry VII sets out to establish himself as the new sovereign, but finds a dangerous, deeply divided kingdom. Lizzie's loyalties are further complicated by the child she is expecting. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.desc: Amy plucks up the courage to audition for a band and a hungover Sean is forced to rethink his life when a friend needs his help. Tim Jenkins, an old friend of Sean's, overdoses.(n)

programme.sub-title: To Catch a Killer - Part Three
programme.desc: Part three of three. A DNA sample seems to rule out cabbie David Russo as the murderer of nurse Manisha Devi - until it transpires that he has cancer and recently underwent a blood transfusion that could have affected the test result. Peter Baxter is stabbed by a traumatised Ruth Cohen, while June has a run-in with a 13-year-old arson suspect and later calls the station to report a rape - naming herself as victim.(n)

Molly is horrified to learn there is a thief on the estate, while Highland Radio Authority officer Parker is on the trail of pirate station Rob Roy Radio. Drama, starring Susan Hampshire, Dawn Steele and Hamish Clark.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The Skelthwaite Scorpions play a crunch match against rival team the Bridlington Trawlers, but fare badly, prompting David to demand a return engagement. Meanwhile, Anna's distress at her split with Simon is compounded by the financial hardship of running a business single-handed _ but just as disaster looms, a shady businessman makes her an offer for the factory. Lesley Dunlop and Philip Middlemiss star.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon spreads her wings and takes full advantage of her sister's hospitality, but Tracey is having money worries. Ross Kemp guest stars, with Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie struggles to come to terms with the news that his prize bull is gay, and Marlene finds she is missing the noise of the city after suffering sleepless nights. Tyler is delighted to learn he has failed all but two of his GCSEs, thinking he will no longer be accepted at a private school - but his parents have other ideas. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean decides to go online, but Lionel is not so sure it is a good idea, and Judith's ex-boyfriend Alistair comes up against a big problem. Comedy about late-blossoming love, with Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy tries to implement a new fitness regime, but Clegg is reluctant to take part and insists some of the world's nicest people are out of shape. Brian Wilde stars.(n)

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Bacchus and Gently arrive in a small town to investigate the apparent suicide of a mill manager who has been found hanged. However, the detectives begin to suspect foul play as they discover money has gone missing and the victim's widow reveals he was having an affair. Starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the killing of a zookeeper, thought to have been mauled to death by a tiger in 2006, when blood found in the victim's lodgings suggests he was murdered before his body was placed in the big-cat enclosure. However, not all the team members are happy about the new case as Brian's animal rights tendencies come to the fore, bringing him into conflict with the zoo's PR. Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman star.(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The body of a Falklands War veteran who became one of the leading lights in the campaign against nuclear weapons is found in a stretch of river running underneath the Ministry of Defence. As Boyd and the team set about investigating the man's former allies, they stumble upon footage of an incident intelligence services had tried to cover up - and Grace is reunited with a former lover from her own time in the anti-nuclear movement. Drama, starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston, with Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe, Into the Storm), John McArdle (Brookside) and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral, Spooks).(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Elizabeth struggles through her labour, while King Henry's envoys embark on a diplomatic mission to Burgundy, a York stronghold abroad. Negotiations are jeopardised by a twist of fate, and Lizzie learns she has more in common with her husband than she first imagined. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: The York princesses are strategically married off to strengthen the Tudor cause, but further political challenges are on the horizon. When King Henry finds himself back on the battlefield to protect his reign, Lizzie learns that the threats they face may be closer to home. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star.(n)

programme.desc: Teenage rapist Ross Trescot is brought into custody, but his arrest does little to comfort June, who insists in being interviewed by Sheelagh, unable to bear the thought of reliving her ordeal with a stranger. programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Paul's errant uncle Donald MacDonald returns to Glenbogle after a 40-year absence, but his homecoming receives a mixed reaction. Lexie sets out to forget her marital problems by organising Molly's 60th birthday party, but the matriarch is not so keen to celebrate. Meanwhile, Isobel faces financial ruin after a contract to supply the estate with fresh bread and vegetables is cancelled, and Jess is concerned that she and Duncan are turning into a boring couple. Drama, guest starring Tom Baker, with Lloyd Owen, Susan Hampshire and Dawn Steele.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Karen is subjected to a horrific assault by the grandson of a pensioner in her care, while Luke gets into trouble at school and responds by running away, plunging Anna into a panic. On a happier note, Tom clinches an important business deal with German client Herr Gruber, while Joe contemplates moving in with Beth. Leslie Ash, Julian Lewis Jones and Lesley Dunlop star.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey makes an appointment with a Harley Street specialist, hoping to allay her suspicions that she may be pregnant. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene discovers the farmhands concocting a virility potion for Rocky the bull, and manages to sneak a dose into Boycie's coffee. Make-up is found in Tyler's room, and his parents are relieved to be told he has a girlfriend - until it transpires she is also his teacher. John Challis, Sue Holderness and Jack Doolan star.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Lynley and Havers investigate when young lawyer Emily Proctor is found axed to death in Hyde Park. They are led to the chambers of a human rights barrister who was having an affair with her, and it isn't long before the victim's seedy double life involving Internet porn and dark family secrets emerges. Detective drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Samuel West.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack announces he is quitting the team, but before Sandra, Brian and Gerry can question his reasons, shadowy Whitehall figure Stephen Fisher arrives with one of his secret cases - the unsolved murder of a woman dating back more than 160 years. With just 24 hours to solve it, will the sleuths find time to get to the bottom of Jack's shock decision and persuade him to stick around?(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team discovers a DNA match between a medical student reported missing six years previously and a corpse that was found at around the same time. programme.desc: The teams embark on the final leg of their journey, heading off on the 4,800 kilometre trek to the finish line in Ushuaia, Argentina, the most southerly city in the world. However, as they prepare to leave the last checkpoint, one of the teams reveals that they have run out of money and will not be completing the challenge. The three remaining pairs rely on different tactics to get ahead, benefiting from risky gambles, lucky opportunities and the kindness of strangers.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality redesigns for a spacious Wiltshire home that used to be a hair salon - so is poorly designed for actually living in, with a ground floor that feels like two separate properties, each with its own front door. Laura gives the building a welcoming entrance while making the interior family-friendly and functional, while Robert comes up with an innovative approach that cuts down on the amount of floor space but which could radically alter the way the owners live.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a liqueur factory in Ireland that produces 540,000 bottles a day. He tracks the journey of a bottle of cream liqueur through the three-year production timeline. At the other end of the journey, Cherry Healey discovers where the empty bottles are recycled. Plus, historian Ruth Goodman visits a monastery to discover the spiritual origins of liqueurs.(n)

programme.title: Hubble: The Wonders of Space Revealed
programme.desc: Since its launch in April 1990, the Hubble space telescope has made more than 1.5 million observations of the universe. One of the most productive scientific instruments in history, over the past three decades it has transformed man's view of the cosmos. This documentary tells the craft's remarkable story, drawing upon contributions by the astronauts who spacewalked on Hubble to carry out critical repairs and upgrades and the astronomers who have used its unique powers to make new discoveries about the universe.(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels from Texas to southern Mexico, meeting a woman who risks her life to take supplies to migrants stranded on the Mexican side of the border in Reynosa, one of the country's most violent cities, which is dominated by a powerful criminal organisation. He also ventures into the rainforest to explore the Maya city of Yaxchilan, and meets the modern-day descendants of this civilisation, who are increasingly marginalised and treated as second-class citizens.(n)

programme.title: The Lake District: A Wild Year
programme.desc: For the wildlife and people who live amid the stunning scenery of the Lake District, life is one of continuous change. This documentary features cutting-edge camera techniques offering a new and unique perspective on a turbulent year in the life of England's largest national park. Time-lapse photography shows months and weeks passing in seconds - snow and ice giving way to sunshine or the frequent rain showers - while the animals, plants and people find extraordinary ways to cope with the challenges of this ancient landscape. Narrated by Bernard Cribbins.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Susan Calman presents as the Welsh chefs serve up their starters and fish courses inspired by children's literature, including an homage to the legend of Twrch Trwyth and an ode to Roald Dahl. The chefs' fate depends on the scores they receive from their veteran and, with one person leaving after the fish course, nerves are running high. With some surprising scores all round, who will be eliminated from the competition and not cook their entire menu? The pressure is on for all the chefs to deliver.(n)

programme.title: Devs
programme.desc: Multiple past outcomes motivate present events as Katie aids Forest's ambitions, while Jamie's loyalty to Lily proves painful. Prior incidents - and their permutations - litter Katie's trawl through events, even those concerning her. Kenton resorts to terror tactics to subdue Jamie, but the security man later makes it clear he will not be held liable for what he has done. The incident in which Forest lost his wife, and daughter Amaya, is key to his desire for Devs to succeed, and Jamie has a decision to make.(n)

programme.title: Mister Winner
programme.desc: As the series concludes, the day of Jemma and Leslie's wedding finally arrives, and all he has to do is get to the venue on time and in one piece. Jemma is very emotional and Leslie is worried she is having second thoughts, so he meets her at the castle gates for a heart to heart. However, disaster soon follows when Leslie gets his hand trapped in a wall, threatening to ruin the entire day.(n)

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: An overweight man turns up at a boxing gym and asks to be turned into a killing machine, much to the exasperation of the trainer. Short buddy comedy, written by and starring Nick Helm, with Lloyd Everitt and Jonathan Pointing.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: The presenter reports on how southern Spain has become the main gateway for drugs into Europe, leading to violent turf wars between gangs and a government crackdown as the police struggle to regain control of their streets. Stacey goes on aerial patrol with police officers guarding the strip of water between Morocco and Spain, and discovers the extraordinary lengths smugglers will take to evade them, including travelling at 100mph in speedboats during extreme weather conditions.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams prepare to leave the checkpoint in Cafayate, Argentina, except for Dom and Lizzie, whose disastrous last leg has left them more than 24 hours behind their competitors. The next destination is the Brazilian island of Ilha Grande, halfway between Rio de Janeiro and Sao Paolo, and at 3,600 kilometres, it is the longest stage of the journey. Emon and Jamiul decide to travel through Paraguay, while Jen and Rob decide they need to see more of the continent, and stop off at the famous Iguazu Falls.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Lesley Sharp narrates the story of a landmark legal battle by a group of women aiming to uncover the truth about birth defects in the Northamptonshire town being caused by toxic waste from the steelworks that had not been disposed of safely. The case had worldwide legal and medical ramifications as it was the first time that a court had recognised a link between airborne toxins and foetal development damage.(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A man fights off masked house raiders to save his wife, a manta ray faces certain death if a fish hook is not removed, and in America, drivers stop to save a woman in a flash flood.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Two divers documenting a wreck in the Thames find more than they bargained for and call in the Royal Navy bomb team. An unsafe load on a vehicle flies off, landing on a windscreen.(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers test out three new products, including a vegan skincare range, a coat for drying dogs, and a device designed to attach items of hand luggage together.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Compton Verney, Warwickshire. Finds include hand-painted fashion illustrations from the 1930s, a pair of bronze cockerels from Benin, West Africa, and a rare Chinese incense burner worth a small fortune. Fiona meets the granddaughter of the first British woman to win an individual swimming gold medal, at the 1924 Olympics, and military expert Mark Smith hears the story of how a First World War hero earned the Victoria Cross.(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble - Into the Volcano
programme.desc: Part one of two. Kate Humble joins a team of geologists on a research expedition to the Vanuatu archipelago in the southern Pacific, beginning at Mount Yasur on the island of Tanna, one of the most active volcanos in the world. To discover whether a devastating eruption might happen, the scientists need to collect a lava bomb the minute it is hurled from the crater and while they prepare for their mission, Kate explores the island and discovers how the volcano shapes the lives, traditions and beliefs of everyone who lives in its shadow.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The mission to find an executive head chef for Lake District restaurant The Gilpin continues. The last three candidates prepare a partridge fit for a fine-dining menu - and with only one place left in tomorrow's final, the pressure is on. While one of the chefs sets out to create something deserving of a Michelin star, another has trouble convincing the family he really wants the position, when it turns out he never stays in one job very long.(n)

programme.title: The War Lords
programme.desc: British historian AJP Taylor appraises the performance of the Second World War leaders, with this edition focusing on the role of Hitler during the conflict.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: John Higgins v Judd Trump. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the 2011 World Championship final, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team is in Old College, Aberystwyth University, on the west coast of Wales, where Christina Trevanion values a pair of Wedgwood plates, while a gold guinea impresses Raj Bisram.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedians Al Murray and Nish Kumar, presenter Anneka Rice and Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills, hosted by Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials.(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard and her guests consider the effects of Covid-19 on the world of literature, asking whether isolation will lead to a post-pandemic rise in books. There is also a discussion on how writing and drama chronicled the HIV and Aids crisis of the 1980s and helped to shape public attitudes.(n)

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Coniston Water isn't the biggest of Cumbria's lakes - but it's one of the most iconic and the place where Donald Campbell died trying to break the world water speed record. Paul meets Campbell's daughter Gina and crew member Robbie Robinson, who was there on the fateful day in January 1967 when Bluebird somersaulted and crashed on the water. The explorer also enters a Lakeland bike ride with a difference before ending his visit with a spot of rock climbing at Dow Crag before the ascent of one of the area's best known hills, The Old Man of Coniston.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Susan Calman presents as the Welsh chefs serve up their mains and desserts inspired by children's literature. The dishes include a Boggis, Bunce and Bean Farmers' Supper based on Fantastic Mr Fox, a guinea fowl inspired by the legend of Welsh dragon Y Ddraig Goch, a Willy Wonka golden egg and a simple marmalade sandwich in homage to everyone's favourite bear from darkest Peru, Paddington.(n)

programme.title: Devs
programme.desc: Stewart feels uptight when he is begged for a favour, especially as he has grave misgivings about Forest and the project. Lily is schooled in the ideas behind Devs, and later - when she emerges from heavy sedation - she realises it is not feasible to flee her problems. Instead she elects to make a house call, but does not get to question the person she expected.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Darren Harriott hosts the show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing stand-up performances by Jessica Fostekew and Stephen Bailey.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Big Animal Surgery
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin enters the world of wild animal surgery, beginning at a sanctuary in South Africa, where she witnesses an extraordinary operation inside the jaws of a lion. Nine-year-old Ricci has problems with his teeth so severe that he is unable to feed properly. One of the world's leading vets is on hand to fix the problem, using an array of specially made tools. But things take a dangerous twist when the patient begins to wake up from the anaesthetic midway through.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a frozen pond surrounded by snow drifts, warmed by a rich sky and late autumn foliage.(n)

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Simon Schama presents a virtual tour of the Young Rembrandt exhibition at the Ashmolean Museum in Oxford, which features works from the first 10 years of the Dutch master's career. He reflects on how Rembrandt's work is particularly relevant to the current crisis, with its portrayal of the fragile nature of human happiness.(n)

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Michael Mosley investigates mankind's earliest attempts to tackle infection and reveals how microbes are used to fight against disease. The programme also examines how scientists, chemists and doctors, from Louis Pasteur to Howard Florey, each contributed to these medical breakthroughs, and there is a look at the eradication of smallpox.(n)

programme.title: Expedition Volcano
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team takes a helicopter to the crater of an active volcano in the Democratic Republic of Congo that has rarely been studied. The mountain is surrounded by forests, which provide cover for armed militant groups, and the scientists will only have a few short hours to gather information. They then move on to the surrounding area, a dangerous landscape marked by craters from past eruptions.(n)

programme.title: Bob Monkhouse: The Last Stand
programme.desc: Cameras capture the quiz show host and comedian in the summer of 2003 - just months before his death - as he entertains a room full of fellow comics with stand-up, chat and a masterclass in his craft. The night became the stuff of legend among comedians, but was never transmitted in full until recently.(n)

programme.title: Talking Comedy
programme.desc: Angela Thorne narrates a look back at clips of the comedian and presenter's appearances on some of the BBC's best-loved talk shows, which have become classic viewing.(n)

programme.title: The Renaissance Unchained
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak returns to Italy to trace the Renaissance from its perceived origins with Giotto and takes a look at the importance of religious narrative in Italian art. He reveals that the majority of Italian painting and sculpture in the 14th and 15th centuries was created to inspire devotion, especially in the work of Piero della Francesca, Michelangelo, Boticelli, Giotto, Leonardo, Raphael and Fra Angelico.(n)

programme.title: The Art That Made Mexico: Paradise, Power and Prayers
programme.desc: Alinka Echeverria argues that indigenous artists not only projected the elites in Mexico's ancient civilisations but became power-brokers in the struggles for political dominance.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a frozen pond surrounded by snow drifts, warmed by a rich sky and late autumn foliage.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures the beauty of the beach as the sun goes down, in a seascape painted in gold, purple and orange.(n)

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Dr James Fox takes viewers on a tour of some of the most profound artworks from Tate Britain's collection, including a self-portrait by 18th-century artist William Hogarth.(n)

programme.title: Michael Wood's Story of England
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores how the villagers reacted to occupation by the Normans in 1066, when a castle was constructed in Kibworth, Leicestershire. Interweaving local and national narratives, he charts events that led to local people's involvement in a civil war, fighting alongside Simon de Montfort.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Mark Chapman narrates an exploration of Britain's complicated relationship with alcohol during the latter half of the 20th century. The programme charts how conventions surrounding the drinking habits of men and women changed from the 1940s onwards, as the largely masculine culture of pubs and working men's clubs began to recede, and also discovers the influence foreign travel and advertising had on the types of beverage that were popular in the UK.(n)

programme.title: Archaeology: A Secret History
programme.desc: Richard Miles continues his series charting the history of archaeological breakthroughs by looking at how discoveries in the 18th and 19th centuries overturned previously formed ideas of when and where civilisation began, as empires competed to make their beliefs prevalent.(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke discover the art and architecture of Barcelona, seeking out the Romanesque frescoes that inspired a young MirÃ³ and the designs of an engineer who arguably put more of a stamp on the city than its star architect, Antoni GaudÃ­.(n)

programme.title: Smokey Robinson Live in Hyde Park
programme.desc: The soul and Motown legend performs a rousing headline set as part of BBC Radio 2's Festival in a Day event, which took place in 2013. Along with a little help from the crowd in London's Hyde Park, Smokey sings a selection of classics from his repertoire, including You've Really Got a Hold on Me, The Tears of a Clown, I Second That Emotion and The Tracks of My Tears.(n)

programme.title: Peter Rice: An Engineer Imagines
programme.desc: A tribute to the life and ideas of Peter Rice, one of the most distinguished structural engineers of the late 20th century. The film traces his career from his native Ireland through London, Sydney and Paris, revealing how he pushed the boundaries of art and science, until to his untimely death in 1992.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another signature landscape, featuring a mountain, cabin and meadow, as seen through a window.(n)

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Art historian Janina Ramirez takes a virtual tour of the British Museum, whose galleries she has paced since she was a girl. Here she highlights objects as varied as an Aztec decorated skull, ancient Egyptian cat mummies and an 18th-century tea set.(n)

programme.title: Great Expectations (2012)
programme.desc: A penniless orphan is chosen by a reclusive spinster to be a playmate for her cold-hearted ward, whom he falls in love with. As he grows up, a mysterious benefactor offers him the chance to rise into high society, but dark secrets lie behind both the object of his adoration and his newfound fortune. Period drama based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring Jeremy Irvine, Helena Bonham Carter, Ralph Fiennes and Holliday Grainger.(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions behind science fiction films, including 2001: A Space Odyssey, Blade Runner, Arrival, Gravity and one of his favourites, Silent Running. He argues that for all their spectacle, sci-fi films ultimately derive their power from being about us.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Scientists and doctors investigate the properties of human hair and examine the methods and results of research done for business that are developing hair care products. programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS: Homes for Veterans - Part One
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team take on their most ambitious project to date and transform a whole street of run-down empty properties in Manchester into homes for veterans. They are joined by the local community, as well as Prince William and Prince Harry, who volunteer their assistance on site. In the first edition of the two-part special, the team builds houses for two former soldiers who fought in Iraq and Afghanistan and are suffering from post-traumatic stress disorder. However, with the number of volunteers lacking and the buildings proving to be trickier than expected, the build is becoming the toughest yet.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Eight celebrities embark on an experimental adventure to India, to see whether they can set up a more rewarding life than in the UK. Enterpreneur Duncan Bannatyne, designer Zandra Rhodes, comedian Paul 'Chuckle' Elliott, cricket legend Henry Blofeld, former EastEnder John Altman, actresses Britt Ekland and Susie Blake and singer Barbara Dickson arrive at their new home by the sea in Puducherry, and while Britt struggles with the heat and Paul misses his missus, Susie, Barbara and Zandra bond over a bit of traditional Indian cooking.(n)

programme.desc: Calum Maclean explores the deep waters of Rubislaw Quarry, hidden away in the centre of Aberdeen. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff checks the secret security camera feed on his phone and is intrigued to see Yasmeen stuffing clothes into a holdall and locking it in a cupboard by the stairs. When Summer explains to Billy that Kelly's mum has abandoned her, he agrees to let her stay at their flat, while Dev agrees to phone the school and try to withdraw his complaint.(n)

programme.title: Absolutely India: Mancs in Mumbai
programme.desc: Brothers Ryan, Adam and Scott Thomas trace their family roots in India, joined by their dad Dougie, whose father was born in Mumbai. However, the trio have never visited the country they descended from. In the opening episode, the siblings arrive in India and have an extremely fiery introduction to the local cuisine. They also give the kids at their grandad's old school a crash course in how to talk like true Mancs, and make a major discovery about the relatives their grandad left behind.(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul O'Grady helps to train a Pomeranian puppy called Stormy who arrived in Battersea at only nine weeks old. Elsewhere, Paul meets a French bulldog in desperate need of an operation to open his airways, and there is also an arrival of 20 dogs who turn up unexpectedly in need of temporary accommodation.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Fly Tipping: Britain's Lockdown Problem? Tonight
programme.desc: Fly tipping costs tens of millions to clean up and is a huge problem across the UK. But it could be getting worse in lockdown. With some council tips shut and many authorities operating a skeleton waste collection service, some people are simply dumping their rubbish illegally. Jonathan Maitland investigates.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.sub-title: The Martin Lewis Money Show - A Coronavirus Special
programme.desc: Everyday life has changed dramatically with everyone facing more restrictions - and more uncertainty. Martin Lewis presents another live special in which he and Angellica Bell will be examining how coronavirus could affect finances, and taking viewers' questions on specific issues.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp: On the NHS Frontline
programme.desc: Ross returns to Milton Keynes Hospital Trust as frontline medical staff brace themselves for the peak of the coronavirus pandemic. With greater numbers of patients testing positive for Covid-19, Kemp witnesses new wards opening to accommodate them and speaks to staff working around the clock to save lives. He also speaks to patients who have come to the hospital with coronavirus symptoms - some will go home and recuperate quickly, but others may have a long road to recovery ahead of them.(n)

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as more studio audience members are called down to play against arguably the most formidable quiz team on TV. First up is a cash builder of five questions, after which they have the option of playing between two, three, four or all of the Chasers for increasing amounts of money - and those brave enough to take on all five could walk away with a huge cash prize.(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents.(n)

programme.title: ITV News London
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? Commissaris Van Der Valk must uncover the truth while also dealing with an over-eager rookie cop joining his tight-knit team. Maimie McCoy, Luke Allen-Gale and Elliot Barnes Worrell co-star.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Fly Tipping: Britain's Lockdown Problem? Tonight
programme.desc: Fly tipping costs tens of millions to clean up and is a huge problem across the UK. But it could be getting worse in lockdown. With some council tips shut and many authorities operating a skeleton waste collection service, some people are simply dumping their rubbish illegally. Jonathan Maitland investigates.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.sub-title: The Martin Lewis Money Show - A Coronavirus Special
programme.desc: Everyday life has changed dramatically with everyone facing more restrictions - and more uncertainty. Martin Lewis presents another live special in which he and Angellica Bell will be examining how coronavirus could affect finances, and taking viewers' questions on specific issues.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp: On the NHS Frontline
programme.desc: Ross returns to Milton Keynes Hospital Trust as frontline medical staff brace themselves for the peak of the coronavirus pandemic. With greater numbers of patients testing positive for Covid-19, Kemp witnesses new wards opening to accommodate them and speaks to staff working around the clock to save lives. He also speaks to patients who have come to the hospital with coronavirus symptoms - some will go home and recuperate quickly, but others may have a long road to recovery ahead of them.(n)

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as more studio audience members are called down to play against arguably the most formidable quiz team on TV. First up is a cash builder of five questions, after which they have the option of playing between two, three, four or all of the Chasers for increasing amounts of money - and those brave enough to take on all five could walk away with a huge cash prize.(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? Commissaris Van Der Valk must uncover the truth while also dealing with an over-eager rookie cop joining his tight-knit team. Maimie McCoy, Luke Allen-Gale and Elliot Barnes Worrell co-star.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Frustrated that a number of cases have been knocked back by the Crown Prosecution Service, Banks and the team are called on to investigate the murder of teenager Ellie Clayton, daughter of a high-profile internet entrepreneur. The victim's background, together with the suggestion she may have been raped, means the incident is soon the subject of much media speculation, and strong evidence implicates a theatre director who ran a drama workshop the girl attended on the night she died. The detective believes he has found his man, but Morton fears her colleague isn't seeing the bigger picture.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The collapse of Owen Pierce's trial has left the case wide open and there's still a killer on the loose, but Banks remains determined to build a watertight legal case against his prime suspect. As the investigation continues, Morton and Jackman uncover promising new leads focusing on Ellie's ex-boyfriend Tyler, but just as they seem to be getting close to the truth, another schoolgirl is murdered, putting Pierce in the spotlight once again.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.sub-title: The Silent World of Nicholas Quinn
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Tired of getting nowhere with Miss Jones, Rigsby switches his amorous attentions to new lodger Marilyn, who turns out to be an exotic dancer - complete with python. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.desc: The local press takes an interest in the missing money and try to interview Alf. Jack discovers Vera hasn't paid the mortgage for two months.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Robertses are horrified when the Gazette accuses Alf of being a thief. Ken is stunned when Tracy discharges herself from hospital. Vicky has a shock in store for Steve.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles are accused of stealing milk, but Zak protests their innocence. Alan tells Frank that only he can restore harmony to the village. Terry gets stood up by Helen.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is still bitter with Frank for letting the Dingles move back into their house. Kathy tries to cheer Nick up. Nellie organises a party to celebrate the Dingles' homecoming.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rivalry between two brass bands gets out of hand and a musician dies of suspected poisoning. Dawn is swept off her feet by a handsome stranger, leaving Gina less than happy when she announces she is moving to Paris with him. Joe and Rachel succumb to their attraction, only for Carol to catch them kissing. Rural drama, guest starring Keith Barron and Peter Wight.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: When the Green Woods Laugh - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop can barely contain his glee as his beloved Mariette's wedding draws closer, until a summons from the magistrate threatens to turn it into a far from 'perfick' day. Whimsical drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Osborne seemingly dies after eating a bowl of Miss Jones' home-made soup, plunging the household into mourning - but Rigsby is the one who ends up being measured for a coffin. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.sub-title: By the Pricking of My Thumbs
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason is called to help Evelyn, a widow who is receiving threats from a corrupt associate of her late husband. Her son-in-law Alan is reluctant to sever his ties with the crook, knowing he provides vital revenue - which forces her into silence. But the police urge Evelyn to cooperate after her car is run off the road and her grandson goes missing. Meanwhile, a Polish visitor arrives in search of a long-lost love.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy warns Nick not to say anything about Dave and Kim's affair. Kim meets Nick and he demands money so that he can support Alice in return for his silence.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim panics when Frank calls the police about Nick. Caroline visits Nick in intensive care. Dave finally tells Kim what he thinks of her.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des takes Andy on a drinking binge to celebrate his 21st birthday. Jack tries to make Vera see she did not kill Cliff. Jamie is suspended from school. Steve proposes to Vicky.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky and Steve try to keep their engagement a secret. Deirdre returns from Morocco after mourning with the Rachids. Raquel organises a party for Andy in the Rovers.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason is called to help Evelyn, a widow who is receiving threats from a corrupt associate of her late husband. Her son-in-law Alan is reluctant to sever his ties with the crook, knowing he provides vital revenue - which forces her into silence. But the police urge Evelyn to cooperate after her car is run off the road and her grandson goes missing. Meanwhile, a Polish visitor arrives in search of a long-lost love.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the death of a racehorse trainer and suspect an aristocratic family's troubled history holds the key to the mystery - until a second killing makes them think again. Groom Jake Foley seems the obvious suspect, but as events unfold, the detectives' inquiries gradually focus instead on the residents of stately Bantling Hall. Detective drama, starring John Nettles.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. A man and woman claiming to be social workers visit a mother and tell her they need to take her son away. However, when they fail to bring him back, it transpires that the pair are not known by the authorities. Banks is drawn into a full-scale search for the missing boy, but new evidence and inconsistent witness statements cause the detective to question the truth of what he has been told. Co-starring Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective now has a murder inquiry on his hands as the body found on the moors is identified as 11-year-old Andre Petri. With Kyle Heath still missing, Banks divides his team to search for a connection between the cases, and when post-mortem examinations show Andre was killed by a heroin overdose, it appears the two boys are linked by the illegal drug trade. Starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan starts dating a posh new girlfriend, but makes the mistake of asking Rigsby for advice on how to conduct himself in polite company - a sure-fire recipe for disaster. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: A Breath of French Air - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dispirited by a wet English summer, the Larkins cross the Channel in the hope that the French way of life will have a beneficial effect on the family. David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones star.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby lets a room to a young couple he believes have just got married - unaware that they are not man and wife. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dawson helps the officers investigate antiques dealer Mr Brigstocke, who is accused of selling stolen goods. Mason and Wetherby stake out the shop to catch him red-handed, but the situation becomes life-threatening when Brigstocke sets fire to it. Meanwhile, prejudices are stirred by the arrival of some Taoist monks and tragedy strikes local troublemakers Julie and Eddie Tinniswood. With Clare Wille and Joe McFadden.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles argue about their new business venture. Nick regains consciousness in the hospital. Kim manipulates Frank into offering Nick his job back when he is better.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy goes to visit Nick in hospital. Tina cleans the house in preparation for inspection. Dave insists he had nothing to do with Nick's accident. Chris asks Rachel to marry him.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve gives Vicky an engagement ring. Don drives the Duckworths to the funeral. Vicky stuns the guests at the party when she announces that she and Steve are engaged.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz doesn't think Steve and Vicky will be happy together. Alec arrives at the Rovers to sort Vicky out. Bet tells him she is certain Steve is after Vicky's money.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dawson helps the officers investigate antiques dealer Mr Brigstocke, who is accused of selling stolen goods. Mason and Wetherby stake out the shop to catch him red-handed, but the situation becomes life-threatening when Brigstocke sets fire to it. Meanwhile, prejudices are stirred by the arrival of some Taoist monks and tragedy strikes local troublemakers Julie and Eddie Tinniswood. With Clare Wille and Joe McFadden.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp Â£5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An idyllic summer day in Oxford is torn asunder when a parcel bomb claims the life of eminent mathematician Adam Capstone, a genius in the field of 'Knot Theory'. As Lewis and Hathaway investigate, they discover that, while the victim's academic reputation was beyond reproach, his private life was far more dubious. It emerges Capstone was a man of carnal appetites, who had a string of affairs with young students, betraying his chemistry professor wife Elizabeth, providing her with a clear motive to do away with her cheating spouse. As the detectives work to identify the killer bomber, the case becomes a personal challenge for Lewis, who fears he may not be rewarded with another contract should he fail to impress Moody before his six-month trip to New Zealand. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox, with Zoe Tapper, Tristam Summers and Oliver Lansley.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.sub-title: Piece of My Heart - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of investigative journalist Matt Barber is found in a holiday chalet in a remote village, Banks and his team are initially confronted by an overwhelming number of leads. As the detective identifies a connection between the victim and a mysterious death in the 1980s connected to the surviving members of a cult rock band, he must begin to unpick decades of lies. To make matters worse, the deceased man's father is former senior police officer Jack Barber, who is now a candidate in the local commissioner elections - and Morton is convinced he is obstructing their inquiry. Stephen Tompkinson stars.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf becomes pompous about his OBE. Tricia keeps her house untidy to put off potential buyers. Vera is furious to discover Jack hasn't paid the council tax for three months.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma forgets her and Mike's anniversary. Martin is given a six-month contract at the hospital. Steve takes Fiona out for an expensive meal with the money Vicky gave him.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric arrives at the village hall to find the Dingles have moved in permanently. Kim goes to see the family solicitor behind Frank's back.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah and Jack prepare to fly out to Spain for Amos and Annie's wedding. Eric despairs at the mess the Dingles are making of his furniture. Kathy and Kim have a confrontation.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe risks his career to help Dawson when she is accused of theft following a raid on local crook Frank Batley's home and Â£400 goes missing. Bernie faces a tough decision when he learns he has a daughter he never knew existed. Starring Joe McFadden and Peter Benson.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: When the Green Woods Laugh - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ma can hardly contain her enthusiasm now that Mariette and Charley have announced their intention to marry. Unfortunately, Pop puts all his finances into bidding for a stately home, casting a shadow over her extravagant plans for the wedding. Rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan starts dating a posh new girlfriend, but makes the mistake of asking Rigsby for advice on how to conduct himself in polite company - a sure-fire recipe for disaster. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rivalry between two brass bands gets out of hand and a musician dies of suspected poisoning. Dawn is swept off her feet by a handsome stranger, leaving Gina less than happy when she announces she is moving to Paris with him. Joe and Rachel succumb to their attraction, only for Carol to catch them kissing. Rural drama, guest starring Keith Barron and Peter Wight.(n)

programme.desc: Rivalry between two brass bands gets out of hand and a musician dies of suspected poisoning. Dawn is swept off her feet by a handsome stranger, leaving Gina less than happy when she announces she is moving to Paris with him. Joe and Rachel succumb to their attraction, only for Carol to catch them kissing. Rural drama, guest starring Keith Barron and Peter Wight.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Frustrated that a number of cases have been knocked back by the Crown Prosecution Service, Banks and the team are called on to investigate the murder of teenager Ellie Clayton, daughter of a high-profile internet entrepreneur. The victim's background, together with the suggestion she may have been raped, means the incident is soon the subject of much media speculation, and strong evidence implicates a theatre director who ran a drama workshop the girl attended on the night she died. The detective believes he has found his man, but Morton fears her colleague isn't seeing the bigger picture.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The collapse of Owen Pierce's trial has left the case wide open and there's still a killer on the loose, but Banks remains determined to build a watertight legal case against his prime suspect. As the investigation continues, Morton and Jackman uncover promising new leads focusing on Ellie's ex-boyfriend Tyler, but just as they seem to be getting close to the truth, another schoolgirl is murdered, putting Pierce in the spotlight once again.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.sub-title: The Silent World of Nicholas Quinn
